whatChanged: Replaced the old Reply Parser / watching_items pattern with a governed action-loop contract over existing Foundation ledgers.
whatItDoes: Promotes the useful old reply intent model, classifies future replies into proposed internal transitions, rejects second private queues, requires owner/evidence, and blocks ambiguous or low-confidence replies from auto-closing work.
whyItMatters: The system can surface intelligence only if it can also tell whether a human handled it. This keeps the loop inside Action Route Review Inbox, decisions, open questions, backlog, synthesized items, and change events instead of creating another hidden queue.
whereItLives: lib/reply-watching-loop.js reply intent classifier, loop snapshot builder, transition validator, and dogfood proof
scripts/process-reply-watching-loop-check.mjs focused process proof and Current Sprint closeout
proofStatus: Focused proof validates approval, Plan Critic, live Action Route Review Inbox reuse, existing ledger presence, forbidden second-queue absence, promoted old reply intents, dogfood rejection cases, closeout registry, and Current Sprint advancement.
reviewNext: Continue STRATEGY-QUARTER-001 with its own scoped plan and proof before building strategy-quarter behavior.
knownLimits: This does not run live Gmail, Missive, Slack, Telegram, or email reply ingestion.
This does not send messages, mutate external systems, rotate credentials, change provider config, mutate Drive permissions, or run paid/provider/browser-auth work.
This does not create a new reply queue table or auto-close ambiguous/low-confidence replies.
This does not build Strategy Hub, People, Harlan, Crewbert, source/extraction, or agent runtime work.

subjectIncludes: Add action route dedupe staleness guard
Action Route dedupe and staleness guard

whatChanged: Added duplicate clustering and staleness policy to the Action Route Review Inbox read model.
whatItDoes: Groups unresolved repeated action-route findings, links route/backlog duplicate rows without data loss, marks stale unresolved findings yellow at three days and red at seven days, and gives every stale or duplicate cluster a next action.
whyItMatters: Steve should not keep seeing the same proposed intelligence pile up without the system naming duplicates, age, and the closure action required.
whereItLives: lib/action-route-dedup-staleness-guard.js dedupe/staleness contract, classifier, validator, and dogfood proof
lib/action-route-review-inbox.js attaches dedupe/staleness to the Review Inbox snapshot
lib/foundation-verifier-control-loop.js verifies the live route carries the dedupe/staleness contract
public/foundation-action-route-review-inbox-renderers.js displays duplicate and stale status without broad UI redesign
proofStatus: Focused proof validates approval, live backlog/current sprint truth, Plan Critic, synthetic duplicate/stale dogfood, Review Inbox snapshot wiring, live verifier coverage, package script, closeout registry, current plan/state, and no extraction/auth/model/external-write tokens.
reviewNext: Continue safe Foundation queue with no-auth source/connector work or other Foundation cleanup from repo truth.
knownLimits: This groups and surfaces duplicates/staleness; it does not automatically reject, snooze, or delete review items.
This does not run live extraction, auth-required or paid jobs, provider/model probes, external writes, Drive permission mutation, or Agent Feedback auto-send.
This does not build Harlan/Fal/voice/Canva/OpenHuman feature work.
This adds small Review Inbox status fields, not a broad visual UI redesign.

subjectIncludes: Add action route Harlan digest packet

whatChanged: Added a no-send Harlan-ready digest packet for the live Action Route Review Inbox.
whatItDoes: Packages live action-route review counts, aged/stale/duplicate signals, and the top bounded review items into a dry-run Harlan message preview with sendsMessageNow=false, externalSent=false, and mutatedRoutes=false.
whyItMatters: The action router already builds owner/reason routes, but route work can still stay invisible. This proves the pending queue can become a clear operator prompt without auto-promoting or sending anything.
whereItLives: lib/action-route-harlan-digest.js digest packet builder, validator, boundaries, and dogfood proof
scripts/process-action-route-harlan-digest-check.mjs focused live-inbox proof and guarded close-card writer
proofStatus: Focused proof validates approval, live digest card state, open parent route-resolution card, synthetic ready/empty/unsafe packet dogfood, live Action Route Review Inbox source, no-send/no-mutation boundaries, and absence of Telegram/send implementation imports.
reviewNext: Continue ACTION-ROUTE-APPLY-RESOLUTION-PROOF-002 for one approved internal pending-to-applied route proof. Continue HARLAN-LIVE-OPERATOR-RUNTIME-002 only if Steve approves broader live Harlan runtime or route-notification delivery.
knownLimits: This is a dry-run packet only; it does not send Telegram, email, Slack, Agent Feedback, or public messages.
This does not approve, apply, reject, snooze, or mutate action routes.
This does not create backlog cards from Director or route recommendations.
This does not start Harlan runtime, reply parsing, login, source-session resume, extraction, provider/model calls, or external-action authority.
The parent ACTION-ROUTE-APPLY-RESOLUTION-PROOF-002 remains open for the actual route approval/apply proof.

subjectIncludes: Add action route apply safety preflight

whatChanged: Added a read-only safety preflight for live action-route apply work.
whatItDoes: Classifies Action Route Review Inbox items into ready, operator-review-required, blocked, or already-resolved states while keeping autoApplyAllowed=false and recording no route, destination, backlog, approval, or external mutation.
whyItMatters: The action-route queue cannot be closed safely by blindly applying recommendations. This gives the operator a source-backed reason for which routes are safe to consider and which must stay parked.
whereItLives: lib/action-route-apply-safety-preflight.js safety classifier, validator, CLI guard verifier, and dogfood proof
scripts/process-action-route-apply-safety-preflight-check.mjs focused live-inbox proof and guarded close-card writer
proofStatus: Focused proof validates approval, live safety-preflight card state, open parent route-resolution card, dogfood ready/pending/sensitive/owner/fail-closed cases, live Action Route Review Inbox source, existing apply CLI confirmation guard, and no approve/apply/reject/send implementation imports in the focused path.
reviewNext: Continue ACTION-ROUTE-APPLY-RESOLUTION-PROOF-002 only with a separately approved live internal route apply or a synthetic transactional proof that does not auto-promote live recommendations.
knownLimits: This is a read-only preflight only; it does not approve, apply, reject, snooze, reroute, or mutate routes.
This does not create decisions, open questions, or backlog cards from Director, Scoper, Portfolio, or route recommendations.
This does not send Telegram, email, Slack, Agent Feedback, or public messages.
This does not start Harlan runtime, reply parsing, login, source-session resume, extraction, provider/model calls, or external-action authority.
The parent ACTION-ROUTE-APPLY-RESOLUTION-PROOF-002 remains open for an actual apply proof after explicit operator approval.

whatChanged: Added a rollback-only synthetic Action Router apply proof that seeds one verified synthesized item and pending action route inside a transaction, calls the real approve/apply functions, verifies destination creation, then rolls the transaction back.
whatItDoes: Proves the queue can move pending to approved to applied through the real internal Action Router path without promoting a live Director, Scoper, Portfolio, or route recommendation.
whyItMatters: Action-route intelligence is only useful if it can resolve into accountable internal work. This closes the code-path proof while keeping live route application as a separate operator-approved step.
whereItLives: lib/action-route-apply-resolution-proof.js synthetic transaction proof and rollback verifier
scripts/process-action-route-apply-resolution-proof-check.mjs focused proof and guarded close-card writer
proofStatus: Focused proof validates approval, live parent card state, real Action Router approve/apply behavior on a synthetic verified route, destination row creation inside the transaction, rollback cleanup counts, package script, verifier coverage, closeout registry, and no external send/source/model tokens.
reviewNext: Do not apply live action routes unattended. Next safe slice is surfacing apply-safety states and digest counts in Dev Hub or Review Inbox so Steve can choose a real route later.
knownLimits: This does not approve, apply, reject, snooze, reroute, or mutate any live action route.
This does not create persistent backlog, decision, or open question records from live recommendations.
This does not send Telegram, email, Slack, Agent Feedback, or public messages.
This does not start Harlan runtime, reply parsing, login, source-session resume, extraction, provider/model calls, or external-action authority.
A real live-route apply still needs explicit operator approval.

whatChanged: Closed the Foundation KB/action-review sprint umbrella after verifying every child card and closeout had shipped.
whatItDoes: Removes ambiguous scoped parent-sprint state while preserving the child closeout sequence and proving no hidden worker, extraction, model, or external-write side effects were introduced.
whyItMatters: The next builder should not have to rediscover that the sprint is complete or risk rerunning approval-bound work from an old umbrella card.
whereItLives: lib/foundation-kb-action-review-sprint.js umbrella evaluator and dogfood proof
scripts/process-foundation-kb-action-review-sprint-check.mjs focused proof and governed close-card writer
proofStatus: Focused proof validates approval, live backlog/current sprint truth, child done-card truth, child closeout coverage, umbrella closeout coverage, package script, current plan/state updates, and dogfood failures for missing child, stale parent, missing closeout, and forbidden runtime side effects.
reviewNext: Continue safe Foundation-up work from repo truth, starting with the source-maturity repair queue if no higher P0 repair appears.
knownLimits: This is an umbrella closeout only; it does not change the KB compiler, Review Inbox, promotion workflow, or dedupe/staleness behavior.
This does not run live extraction, auth-required or paid jobs, provider/model probes, external writes, Drive permission mutation, or Agent Feedback auto-send.
This does not launch hidden workers or delegated subagents.
This does not build Harlan/Fal/voice/Canva/OpenHuman feature work.
